questions.question: Explain the difference between a singly linked list and a self-referential structure. Provide an example to illustrate your answer.
questions.hint: A self-referential structure is a data structure where each node contains a reference to another node.

questions.question: What is the time complexity of traversing a singly linked list using a loop versus recursion? Provide examples to support your answer.
questions.hint: The time complexity of traversing a singly linked list using a loop is O(n), whereas the time complexity of traversing a singly linked list using recursion is O(n) due to the call stack.

questions.question: Explain the concept of a null node in a singly linked list. Provide an example of how it is used in practice.
questions.hint: A null node is a node that serves as the terminator of a linked list, indicating the end of the list.
